key_insight: AA-ViT uses anatomical, structural, and frequency guidance in a vision transformer to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI from pre-contrast scans while better preserving tumour and tissue boundaries.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this could improve lesion visibility without gadolinium, potentially reducing cost, scan time, and contrast-agent risk for some brain MRI patients.
method: Inputs T1, T2, and FLAIR pre-contrast MRI
Vision transformer guided by anatomy plus structural/frequency cues
Designed to improve boundary and fine-detail preservation in CEMRI synthesis
evidence_signal: On BraTS 2021, the method outperformed state-of-the-art approaches on PSNR and SSIM, and 3 clinicians scored 19 randomly selected cases a mean of 3.94/5.
watch_item: Clinical use still needs broader prospective validation, especially to confirm performance across scanners, tumour types, and real-world workflows.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
